template_name: Standard Prescription Format – Umbilical Cord Care
diagnosis: Normal umbilical cord healing / Umbilical cord care counseling
Parental education provided regarding cord care

normal_findings: Minimal bleeding for 1–2 days post detachment

general_care: Keep umbilical cord stump clean and dry
Clean only with clean water if soiled
Allow air exposure; do not cover the stump
Fold diaper below stump to keep it dry
Do not pull or forcibly detach the stump
Continue sponge bath until cord falls off

antiseptic_use.routine: Not required in most settings (dry cord care preferred)
antiseptic_use.high_risk_setting: Apply 4% chlorhexidine as per WHO recommendation

what_to_avoid: Application of ash, oil, turmeric, ghee, cow dung, or herbal pastes
Covering the stump tightly with cloth
Pulling on the stump to hasten separation
Routine use of alcohol or antiseptics unless indicated

management_based_on_findings.action: Reassure parents and continue dry cord care
Observe and clean gently with sterile/clean water
Silver nitrate cauterization if no infection

red_flags: Redness extending beyond base of stump
Cord not falling off by 3–4 weeks
parent_counseling: Cord will dry and fall off on its own; do not pull it — नाल सूखकर खुद गिर जाएगी; खींचें नहीं
Keep the area clean and dry at all times — जगह को हमेशा साफ़ और सूखा रखें
Fold diaper below the stump to avoid moisture — डायपर को नाल के नीचे मोड़कर रखें
Avoid any home remedies or powders — कोई घरेलू उपचार या पाउडर न लगाएं
Mild discharge or bleeding after fall is normal — नाल गिरने के बाद हल्का रिसाव या खून सामान्य है
Seek medical help if redness, pus, foul smell, fever, or excessive crying occurs — लालिमा, पीप, बदबू, बुखार या ज़्यादा रोना हो तो डॉक्टर को दिखाएं

early_review_if: Baby becomes irritable, lethargic, or feeds poorly

warning_signs: Redness extending beyond base of stump — नाल के आस-पास लालिमा फैलना
Purulent or foul-smelling discharge — पीप या बदबूदार रिसाव
Fever, lethargy, poor feeding — बुखार, सुस्ती, दूध न पीना
Cord not falling off by 3-4 weeks — 3-4 हफ़्ते में नाल न गिरना
expected_course: The umbilical cord stump dries and separates naturally within 5-15 days. Color changes from white to yellow to brown/black before falling off. Mild serous ooze after separation is normal. Delayed separation beyond 3 weeks may indicate infection or immune deficiency and warrants evaluation.
key_clinical_points: Dry cord care is the standard of care (WHO recommendation)
4% chlorhexidine application recommended only in high-risk/community settings
Traditional practices (oil, turmeric, ghee, ash) increase infection risk
Delayed separation (>3 weeks) may indicate leukocyte adhesion deficiency
Umbilical granuloma (persistent red nodule) treated with silver nitrate cauterization
Omphalitis signs require urgent referral and IV antibiotics

severity_assessment.mild: Normal drying cord, no discharge, no redness — dry cord care, reassurance
severity_assessment.moderate: Minor serous ooze, mild periumbilical redness, delayed separation — close follow-up, chlorhexidine if needed
severity_assessment.severe: Purulent discharge, spreading erythema, systemic signs (fever, lethargy) — urgent referral for omphalitis management
